# Chaos Report — bronze.eada (raw zone)

**Spec:** `docs/specs/full-pipeline-eada.md` §4 DQ Rules + §8 amendment (RAW-EAD-012).
**Rule set under test:** `governance/dq-rules/raw-eada.json` — 12 rules (RAW-EAD-001 .. RAW-EAD-012).
**Target table:** `bronze.eada` at `data/bronze/iceberg_warehouse/bronze/eada/`.
**Baseline:** 2,040 rows, 2,040 distinct UNITIDs, single `reporting_year=2022`, all monetary fields 100% non-null.
**Generated:** 2026-04-30
**Generated by:** @chaos-monkey
**Verdict:** **GO** — every targeted attack was caught; only out-of-rule-set dimensions (reasonableness upper-bound, freshness on `ingested_at`) slipped through, both already declared out-of-scope by the EDA. Recommendations filed below.

---

## 1. Methodology

Because no chaos-monkey infrastructure or in-pipeline DQ runner exists yet on this branch, the campaign was run via a self-contained harness at `/tmp/chaos-eada/chaos_harness.py`:

1. The on-disk parquet at `data/bronze/iceberg_warehouse/bronze/eada/data/*.parquet` is read into pandas (read-only).
2. For each cycle / attack, a fresh in-memory copy is corrupted, registered into a fresh DuckDB connection as `bronze.eada`, and every rule's SQL from `governance/dq-rules/raw-eada.json` is executed.
3. Pass/fail is decided per the rule's `threshold` field: `result = 0` for scalar `violation` rules and `result_count = 0` for "violator-rows" rules.
4. The on-disk parquet is **never written to**. Restoration is therefore trivial — there was nothing to undo. MD5 of the parquet was identical pre- and post-campaign (`16948b7cd2801f9ac3513415416e64b7`).

**Pre-flight:** all 12 rules pass against the unmodified table.
**Post-flight:** all 12 rules still pass against the unmodified table (re-loaded fresh from disk).

---

## 2. Cycles & attacks executed (11 total)

### 5 escalating broad-spectrum cycles

| Cycle | Injection rate | Total injections | Caught | Missed |
|------:|---------------:|-----------------:|-------:|-------:|
| 1     | 5%             | 11               | 9      | 2 (out-of-scope) |
| 2     | 6%             | 11               | 9      | 2 (out-of-scope) |
| 3     | 7%             | 11               | 9      | 2 (out-of-scope) |
| 4     | 8%             | 11               | 9      | 2 (out-of-scope) |
| 5     | 10%            | 11               | 9      | 2 (out-of-scope) |

Each broad cycle injected: NULL unitid, NULL on each of the three monetary fields (sized to push below 99%), -1 sentinel on each monetary field, full-row duplicates (uniqueness), reporting_year leak, $10T outlier, and stale `ingested_at`. The last two are intentional probes against dimensions the rule set does not cover (see §4 gaps).

### 6 targeted attacks (per spec)

| # | Attack | Rules expected to fire | Rules that fired | Caught? |
|--:|--------|------------------------|------------------|:-------:|
| 1 | **Per-team leak** — fan-out 150 institutions across 4 fake sports → +600 rows, all duplicating UNITIDs | RAW-EAD-001, RAW-EAD-003, RAW-EAD-012 | RAW-EAD-001 (row count 2,640 > 2,300), RAW-EAD-003 (150 dup keys), **RAW-EAD-012 (the new tripwire — \|2640 − 2040\| = 600, far exceeds 1% tolerance of 20)** | YES |
| 2 | **Suppression-sentinel leak** — 25 cells/field coerced→NULL (legit ingestor path), 5 cells/field with raw `-1`/`-2` leak | RAW-EAD-004/005/006 (validity), RAW-EAD-007/008/009 (completeness pushed below 99%) | All six fired: 5 negative violators per field caught by 004/005/006; non-null fraction 2010/2040 = 98.5% < 99% caught by 007/008/009 | YES |
| 3 | **Negative monetary values** — 10 plain negatives in each of the three money fields | RAW-EAD-004/005/006 | All three fired (10 violators each) | YES |
| 4 | **UNITID type corruption** — 5 NULL UNITIDs (simulating quoted-string coercion failure) + 9 collisions to a single UNITID (simulating float→int rounding) | RAW-EAD-002, RAW-EAD-003 | RAW-EAD-002 (5 null violators), RAW-EAD-003 (the surviving collision UNITID has count=10) | YES |
| 5 | **Multi-year leak** — 8 rows set to `reporting_year=2021`, 8 rows to `2023` | RAW-EAD-010 | RAW-EAD-010 fired (3 distinct values: 2021, 2022, 2023) | YES |
| 6 | **Anchor failure** — remove all 60 rows with `total_athletic_expenses > $100M` | RAW-EAD-011 (P1 plausibility) | RAW-EAD-011 fired (zero rows above $100M). Note: row count drops to 1,980 — still inside RAW-EAD-001's [1800, 2300] floor, so RAW-EAD-001 correctly does **not** fire. RAW-EAD-011 catches what RAW-EAD-001 was deliberately scoped not to. | YES |

**Targeted attack score: 6/6 caught.**

---

## 3. Caught vs Undetected — full tally

| Bucket | Count |
|---|---:|
| Total injections (across all 11 cycles/attacks) | **69** |
| Caught by at least one expected rule | **59** (85.5%) |
| Undetected | **10** (14.5%) |

### Undetected injections (all from broad-spectrum cycles, all out-of-rule-set dimensions)

| Cycles | Injection | Dimension | Why undetected |
|-------|-----------|-----------|----------------|
| 1–5   | `outlier_expenses` ($10T per row, 8–17 rows) | reasonableness | Rule set has no upper-bound validity rule on monetary fields. EDA observed max = $234M (Ohio State); a $10T row is implausible by 4 orders of magnitude. **Recommend new rule (see §4).** |
| 1–5   | `stale_ingested_at` (1999-01-01) | freshness | Rule set has no freshness rule on `ingested_at` or `load_date`. **Recommend new rule (see §4).** |

Every other corruption — every UNITID attack, every monetary-validity attack, every completeness attack, every consistency attack, the per-team leak, the multi-year leak, the anchor-failure attack — was caught.

---

## 4. Gap analysis & rule recommendations

The chaos campaign exposed two coverage gaps that align with two of the ten DQ dimensions the rule set does not currently exercise. Both are P2-grade additions (the existing rules already cover the P0/P1 failure modes the spec scoped). Filing them here for @dq-rule-writer triage rather than blocking governance review on them, because:

- The spec §4 rule list (RAW-EAD-001..012) is fully implemented and battle-tested.
- The two gaps catch only contrived adversarial probes; neither corresponds to a known EADA failure mode in the source-system literature.

### Recommended rule: RAW-EAD-013 (proposed, P2, Reasonableness)

```sql
-- total_athletic_expenses upper-bound plausibility
-- EDA observed max = $234,409,941 (Ohio State).
-- An order-of-magnitude headroom guards against unit errors (cents-as-dollars,
-- thousands-vs-actual) without binding on legitimate D1-FBS growth.
SELECT * FROM bronze.eada
 WHERE total_athletic_expenses > 1000000000  -- $1B
```

Threshold: `result_count = 0`. Rationale: EDA P95=$50M, P99=$120M, max=$234M. A $1B threshold gives ~4x headroom over the largest legitimate reporter and would catch unit errors and adversarial outliers like the $10T probe. Note: same recommendation should apply to `total_athletic_revenue` (would be RAW-EAD-014).

### Recommended rule: RAW-EAD-015 (proposed, P2, Freshness)

```sql
-- ingested_at must be within the last 18 months of the current run.
-- 18 months covers normal annual refresh + delayed re-runs.
SELECT * FROM bronze.eada
 WHERE ingested_at < (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL 18 MONTH)
    OR ingested_at > CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
```

Threshold: `result_count = 0`. Rationale: catches both stale data (ingestor reading from a stale cache) and clock-skew futures.

These three recommendations are filed but **not blocking**. The 12-rule set as constituted catches every spec-scoped failure mode plus all 6 targeted attacks the campaign was instructed to land. **Bronze is GO for governance review.**

---

## 5. Per-rule fire-rate across all 11 cycles

| Rule | Priority | Fired in N cycles | Notes |
|------|:--------:|------------------:|-------|
| RAW-EAD-001 (volume) | P0 | 1 | per-team leak only — anchor-failure correctly stayed within [1800, 2300] |
| RAW-EAD-002 (unitid non-null) | P0 | 6 | every cycle that nulls UNITID |
| RAW-EAD-003 (unitid unique) | P0 | 7 | every cycle that adds dups + UNITID collision attack |
| RAW-EAD-004 (expenses ≥ 0) | P0 | 7 | every neg/sentinel attack |
| RAW-EAD-005 (revenue ≥ 0) | P0 | 7 | every neg/sentinel attack |
| RAW-EAD-006 (recruiting ≥ 0) | P0 | 7 | every neg/sentinel attack |
| RAW-EAD-007 (expenses 99% complete) | P0 | 6 | every cycle that nulls expenses |
| RAW-EAD-008 (revenue 99% complete) | P0 | 6 | every cycle that nulls revenue |
| RAW-EAD-009 (recruiting 99% complete) | P1 | 6 | every cycle that nulls recruiting |
| RAW-EAD-010 (single year) | P0 | 6 | every cycle that leaks a year |
| RAW-EAD-011 (D1 anchor) | P1 | 1 | anchor-failure attack only |
| RAW-EAD-012 (row-count ≈ distinct UNITIDs) | P0 | 6 | every dup-introducing cycle including the per-team leak |

Every rule fired at least once. **No dead rules.** RAW-EAD-012 — the new §8-amendment tripwire — fired exactly when expected, including the per-team leak attack it was specifically commissioned to catch.

---

## 6. Restoration verification

- On-disk parquet MD5 pre-campaign: `16948b7cd2801f9ac3513415416e64b7`
- On-disk parquet MD5 post-campaign: `16948b7cd2801f9ac3513415416e64b7` (identical)
- Re-loaded baseline row count: 2,040
- Re-loaded distinct UNITIDs: 2,040
- Re-loaded distinct reporting_year: {2022}
- Re-loaded baseline DQ rule run: **12/12 PASS**

The harness was deliberately read-only against disk (in-memory corruption only), so "restoration" is a no-op. Verified by re-running every rule against a freshly-read parquet at the end of the campaign — all 12 pass.

---

## 7. GO / NO-GO

**GO.** Bronze EADA is hardened sufficiently for governance review. All 12 published rules fire when expected, no false positives in the post-flight, all 6 targeted attacks landed exactly where the spec said they should. Two coverage gaps (upper-bound reasonableness, ingested_at freshness) are filed as P2 follow-ups — they are gaps in dimension coverage, not gaps in spec-scoped rule effectiveness.
